history.content:
<thought>
I am currently at position D7, facing forward. The cell directly in front of me (D6) is lava, which is deadly and cannot be crossed. To avoid lava, I will need to turn away from the forward direction. Since cells to the right (E7) and forward-right (E6) appear to be safe floor cells, turning right would be a good choice to move towards the safer floor cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing upwards at position D7. The cell right in front (D6) is lava, so moving forward is deadly. The agent needs to find a safe path around the lava. To the left (C7) is a wall at C7 (no, at C7 there's 'L' lava) and to the right (E7) is a wall (#). So turning left or right won't help move forward safely. The best move is to turn right (clockwise) to face right (east), where the path may be open after rotating. Then the agent can try to move forward if safe.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at row 07, column D, and the cell in front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To navigate, I will turn right to find a path, as turning left would lead me alongside the wall and possibly dead ends.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In front of me (row 7, column D) is an empty floor cell, safe to move forward. However, directly to the right (column E) is lava. Moving forward is safe and will advance me in the grid, which is preferable to turning since I currently don't see the goal and want to explore more space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The path ahead is blocked by a wall (#) at C6 and D6, and there is lava (L) to the immediate right front at E7. Moving forward is blocked by walls, and moving forward would lead to lava. It would be safer to turn left to explore other directions and avoid lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing upwards, and the cell in front at D6 is a wall `#`, which I cannot move into. To progress, I need to turn either right or left to face a direction with an open floor cell. Looking to the left (west) at C7 and to the right (east) at E7, C7 is floor `.` and seems clear. Turning left will allow me to move toward open space.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing upward (north) with a wall at column E blocking the path forward. The cells directly ahead are clear floors on columns A to D, except column C which has lava. Since the agent is blocked by a wall forward, I should turn left or right to navigate around the obstacle. Turning left will orient me west towards column C which has lava, risky. Turning right will orient me east towards column E which is a wall, so that's not an option. Therefore, turning left might be better to then carefully move forward to avoid lava or find a different safe path around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing forward at the bottom-center of the view, with the cell in front being a wall (`#`). Walls are impassable, so moving forward is not possible. To find a way around the wall, I should turn either left or right. From the observation, turning left or right might open a path since the right and left columns have open floor cells (`.`). I will turn left to explore that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT

<thought>

</thought>
